step_rationale: Decreased lysophosphatidylcholine predicts post-MI mortality; myocardial injury and inflammation marker.
mechanism: Decreased LPC (18:0) content post-MI reflects phospholipid catabolism linked to myocardial injury and inflammatory response.

references.title: Lipidomic analyses reveal potential biomarkers for predicting death and heart failure after acute myocardial infarction.
references.journal: Clinica chimica acta; international journal of clinical chemistry
